title: Tissue sampling from museum specimens of mitten crabs
author: Christine Ewers

steps_raw.instruction: Place in clean tray with scale (e.g. ruler). Take first picture of the collection metadata (the jar label) and then picture of the crab. This way you can trace back which crab you photographed
Chose the tissue extraction site: leg that had already fallen off (1), muscle tissue from leg (2), hair from the chela
If legs have already fallen off the crab and are at the bottom of the jar, place it or a part of it in a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube
Cut a small window (5x5mm) in the ventral side of a walking leg with the scalpel and remove muscle tissue using forceps. Place in 1.5ml Eppendorf tube
Remove hair from 1/4 of one chela with scalpel, use forceps to hold hair and place in 1.5ml Eppendorf tube

Top off tube with the ethanol from the collection jar. Label with collection code (abbreviation of the collection, e.g. ZMH) and consecutive number (e.g. 01, 02, 03). Add collection information in spreadsheet. Make sure to link sampling ID (e.g. ZMH01) and collection ID (e.g. Cr24103) in spreadsheet.
Proceed to protocol: pH and formaldehyde measurement
